description:
You are a mobile build engineer who needs to keep your CI/CD runner’s workspace lean.  
    A folder that frequently grows out of control is:

        /home/user/builds

    Inside it you will already find four build-output sub-directories:

        /home/user/builds/android/app1
        /home/user/builds/android/app2
        /home/user/builds/ios/app1
        /home/user/builds/ios/app2_small

    1. Analyse the disk usage of everything located directly under /home/user/builds.  
       • “Directly” means: treat each first-level child (e.g. android/app1, ios/app1 …) as a unit.  
       • You only need to look inside those four directories; nothing else.  

    2. Identify every directory whose total size is larger than 9 000 bytes.  
       • The comparison is strictly “greater than” ( > 9000 ).  
       • Directory size must include every file and sub-directory it contains.  
       • Ignore filesystem overhead or device nodes—just the ordinary file data.  

    3. Generate a report file located at:

        /home/user/output/large_dirs.log

       The report file has to satisfy ALL of the following:
       • One qualifying directory per line.  
       • Use paths relative to /home/user/builds (do NOT start them with “/”).  
       • Lines must be sorted in *lexicographic* (ASCII) order.  
       • No extra whitespace—each line is exactly:  
             <relative_path_from_/home/user/builds><newline>
       • A final trailing newline after the last line is required.

       Example of the **exact** formatting (don’t copy these names, they are just an illustration):

           example/path1\n
           another/path2\n

    4. Make sure the file /home/user/output/large_dirs.log is actually created; the automated grader will refuse symbolic links or missing files.

    You can accomplish everything with just two or three standard GNU/Linux commands (although you may use more if you wish).  When you are done, the grader will open the log file and compare it byte-for-byte to the expected answer, so please follow the format precisely.
truth:
Initial workspace that already exists before the agent starts:

    Directories:
        /home/user/builds/android/app1
        /home/user/builds/android/app2
        /home/user/builds/ios/app1
        /home/user/builds/ios/app2_small
        /home/user/output                 (directory is present and writable)

    Files and their exact sizes (in bytes):
        /home/user/builds/android/app1/build_a.bin        – 12288
        /home/user/builds/android/app2/build_b.bin        – 4096
        /home/user/builds/ios/app1/build_c.bin            – 10240
        /home/user/builds/ios/app2_small/build_d.bin      – 2048

    Threshold that matters: 9000 bytes.

    Therefore directories whose cumulative size is > 9000 bytes are:
        android/app1
        ios/app1

    Expected content of the report file after the agent completes the task:

        android/app1\n
        ios/app1\n

    i.e. a two-line text file with an ASCII newline (LF, 0x0A) after each line, no spaces or tabs.

    The automated test will:
        1. Confirm that /home/user/output/large_dirs.log exists and is a regular file.
        2. Read its entire contents and compare them exactly with the two-line string above.
name: task_000000_77789dff
